name: PricingCardOne
description: Pricing card with badge, price, subtitle, and feature list with checkmarks.
details: Use for displaying pricing plans with clear feature breakdowns. Layout: Badge (with optional icon) → Price (large, bold) → Subtitle (plan description) → Divider → Feature List (with check icons). All cards must have badge, subtitle, and features for visual consistency. Best for pricing, subscription plans, or tiered services. Automatically switches between grid (1-4 items) and carousel (5+ items). Optional header with title, description, tag, and buttons.

description.example: Select the perfect plan for your needs

badge.note: Required for visual consistency across all cards

subtitle.note: Required for visual consistency across all cards

features.note: Array of feature strings, required for visual consistency

badgeIcon.note: Optional Lucide icon to display next to badge

propsSchema.plans: Array<{ id: string, badge: string, badgeIcon?: LucideIcon, price: string, subtitle: string, features: string[] }>
propsSchema.title: string
propsSchema.titleSegments?: Array<{ type: 'text', content: string } | { type: 'image', src: string, alt?: string }> - For inline images in title
propsSchema.buttons?: Array<{text: string, onClick?: () => void, href?: string}>
propsSchema.textboxLayout: 'default' | 'split' | 'split-actions' | 'split-description' | 'inline-image' (required - 'inline-image' uses titleSegments for rich text with images)
propsSchema.textBoxTitleImageWrapperClassName?: string - For styling image wrapper in inline-image layout
propsSchema.textBoxTitleImageClassName?: string - For styling images in inline-image layout

usageExample: <PricingCardOne plans={[{ id: '1', badge: 'Most Popular', price: '$29/mo', subtitle: 'Perfect for small teams', features: ['Up to 10 team members', '100GB storage', 'Priority support'] }]} title="Choose Your Plan" description="Find the perfect plan for your needs" textboxLayout="default" animationType="slide-up" useInvertedBackground={false} />
